narration: You built an emergency fund — that's a big deal. But almost nobody tells you the most expensive mistake people make: where they're keeping it.
visual: Person silhouette next to a piggy bank. Red dollar coins drain downward out of the coin slot while the person watches. Headline: YOUR EMERGENCY FUND IS WRONG.

narration: Your regular savings account pays just 0.46% interest right now. High-yield savings accounts pay around 4.5% — that's nearly ten times more, same insurance.
visual: Two side-by-side bar charts growing — a short red bar labeled 0.46% (Regular Savings) and a tall green bar labeled 4.5% (High-Yield). '10X MORE INTEREST' headline.

narration: On a $20,000 emergency fund over five years, that gap adds up to $3,400 your bank keeps instead of you. That's money you earned and then just gave away.
visual: Two value boxes side by side: $20,460 in red (Regular Savings) vs $23,860 in green (HYSA). A counter counts up to $3,400 in a big highlight box labeled YOU'RE MISSING.

narration: Here's what's wild — high-yield savings accounts are completely free to open, take about ten minutes, and your money stays FDIC insured the whole time.
visual: Three cards pop in one by one: FREE (no fees), FDIC (federally insured shield icon), 10 MIN (clock icon to set up). Light background with green accents.

narration: Every month you leave your emergency fund in a regular account, your bank pockets that difference. They're making billions off people's idle savings every year.
visual: Dollar signs float from a small piggy bank on the left into a growing bank building on the right. The bank building expands as more dollars flow in. Dark background.

narration: Search 'high-yield savings account' right now. Ten minutes, zero fees, same protection — and over five years, it puts $3,400 back in your pocket, not theirs.
visual: A search bar showing 'high-yield savings account'. Rising green bar chart below it. Green CTA box: '10 MINUTES COULD EARN YOU $3,400'. Light background.
